title: The Instagram Carousel Is Quietly Outperforming Every Other Format
author: Selina Fan
excerpt: Everyone's obsessed with Reels. Meanwhile, carousels are generating 3x the saves and 2x the shares. Here's why — and exactly how we structure ours.
body:
## Reels Get Reach. Carousels Get Results.

Reels are great for discovery. But when we look at the metrics that actually matter for business — saves, shares, profile visits, and link clicks — **carousels win every time**.

Across our client accounts, carousels generate:
- 3.1x more saves than Reels
- 2.2x more shares than static posts
- 40% higher profile visit rate

## Why Carousels Work

A carousel is a micro-story. The swipe mechanic creates commitment — once someone swipes to slide 2, they're 80% likely to reach the end. That sustained attention is gold for both the algorithm and your message.

## Our 6-Slide Formula

After testing hundreds of carousels, here's the structure that consistently performs:

1. **Hook slide** — Bold statement or question that creates curiosity
2. **Problem slide** — Validate the pain point your audience feels
3. **Insight slide** — Share something surprising or counterintuitive
4. **Solution slides (2-3)** — Deliver actionable value
5. **CTA slide** — Tell them exactly what to do next (save, share, follow, link in bio)

## Design Tips That Move the Needle

- Use a consistent visual system (fonts, colours, layout) so your carousels are instantly recognisable
- Keep text to 30 words or fewer per slide
- Use the first slide as if it were a Reel thumbnail — it needs to stop the scroll
- Add subtle visual cues (arrows, "swipe" text) on slides 1-2 to train the behaviour

## The Takeaway

Don't abandon Reels — but if you're not posting carousels weekly, you're leaving engagement on the table. They're the highest-intent format on Instagram, and they compound over time as people save and revisit them.
